channel.title: Bogdan Penkovsky, PhD on Bogdan Penkovsky, PhD
channel.description: Recent content in Bogdan Penkovsky, PhD on Bogdan Penkovsky, PhD

item.title: Delay Differential Equations
item.description:
DDE package is a fast library written in Haskell that I have extracted after my PhD. My work involved a lot dynamical systems described by DDEs. I kept switching between several tools, still I had no fast and flexible library. So here is one now.

Here is an example of a DDE model with two delays from a recent publication:

-- Equation right-hand side
rhs phi0 = DDE.RHS derivative
  where
    derivative ((V2 x y), (DDE.Hist histSnapshots), _) = V2 x' y'
      where
        -- DDE Eq. (4) from arXiv:1712.03283
        x' = (-x - delta * y + (1 - gamma) * f x_tau1 + gamma * f x_tau2) / epsilon
        y' = x

        f = airy phi0

        -- Delay terms where tau2 / tau1 = 100
        (V2 x_tau1 _):(V2 x_tau2 _):_ = histSnapshots

        -- Constants
        epsilon = 0.01
        gamma = 0.5
        delta = 0.009

The complete model is available here.

item.title: Welcome
item.description: I am pleased to welcome you on my blog page.

item.title: Hmep
item.description: Multi Expression Programming is a genetic programming variant encoding multiple solutions in the same chromosome. Essentially, a chromosome is a computer program consisting of reusable subprograms. The remarkable thing about MEP algorithms is that they are programs actually writing programs.
